text: As I stand here, bathed in colored light, everything feels artificial and slightly alien. The glass, the neon, the displays—each is carefully arranged to evoke a sense of progress and inquiry. Yet, beneath the surface, I feel a sense of distance. The environment is stimulating and modern, but I am aware of my own disconnection, as if I am an observer rather than a participant. There is excitement in the air, a hum of possibility, but also a hovering uncertainty—what, in all this striving and research, are we really seeking?
